question: When is running your own inference/platform worth the ops cost vs using a managed API/cloud?
core_principle: Self-host trades ops burden for control + unit-cost-at-scale; managed trades $/token for zero ops. The crossover is a function of two axes: VOLUME (does fixed GPU+ops cost beat per-token API spend?) and COMPLIANCE (does data-residency / air-gap forbid the managed path outright?). Compliance is a gate (binary, overrides cost); volume is a slider (continuous crossover).

operation_candidates.name: Volume-crossover calculation
operation_candidates.trigger: Choosing where to run inference and cost is a stated pressure; you have (or can estimate) a monthly token / request volume.
action: Estimate monthly volume: requests/mo x (avg input + output tokens).
Managed cost = volume x blended $/token (use the provider's input+output mix for your model).
Self-host cost = amortized GPU (rent or own) + ops labor (DevOps fraction-of-FTE) + storage/networking + the long tail (backup, upgrades, on-call).
Crossover volume V* = (GPU + ops + infra fixed cost) / (managed $/token - self-host marginal $/token).
Decide: below V* managed wins on total cost; above V* self-host wins IF utilization stays high (idle GPUs invert the math).
operation_candidates.output: A crossover volume V* and which side of it the projected volume sits on, with the ops-labor term made explicit (not assumed zero).
evidence: output/dify-sop-skill/SKILL.md Case 3 (用量大 millions of calls -> self-host; team <=3 small usage -> Cloud $59-159/mo beats养 DevOps)
https://www.architjn.com/blog/dify-cloud-pricing-plans-free-tier-when-to-self-host

operation_candidates.name: Compliance / data-residency gate
operation_candidates.trigger: Data touched by the workload is regulated, customer-confidential, or contractually bound to a region/boundary.
action: Ask: does any law / contract / certification (HIPAA, finance, GDPR residency, air-gap, gov) forbid data leaving your boundary?
If YES -> compliance gate FAILS the managed path regardless of volume; self-host (or a BAA-backed managed offering in-region) is mandatory.
If a managed provider offers an in-region / VPC / BAA tier that satisfies the requirement, that re-opens the managed path - check before defaulting to self-host.
Treat this as a binary gate evaluated BEFORE the volume slider: a hard compliance NO overrides any cost advantage.
operation_candidates.output: A pass/fail on the managed path from compliance alone, evaluated before cost.
evidence: output/dify-sop-skill/SKILL.md Case 3 (合规/数据驻留 金融医疗 -> self-host; air-gapped/内网 -> self-host)
output/vllm-sop-skill/SKILL.md OP-4 caveat (multi-tenant cache salting / cross-tenant privacy)

operation_candidates.name: Honest ops-capacity check
operation_candidates.trigger: Self-host looks attractive on the cost slider; before committing, validate you can actually run it.
action: Self-host is NOT 'docker compose up' done - it is reverse proxy + HTTPS + backups + monitoring + upgrade strategy + on-call.
Score the team honestly: do you have DevOps capacity / SRE on-call / a person who owns the GPU box at 3am?
Production self-host means external PostgreSQL/Redis/Vector DB (not the bundled containers), K8s + Helm or equivalent, and a tested upgrade path.
If ops capacity is absent, the 'cheaper at scale' GPU math is a trap: outage cost + opportunity cost of the team's time erases the savings.
operation_candidates.output: A go/no-go on self-host based on real ops capacity, with the unbundling-of-stateful-stores requirement flagged.
evidence: output/dify-sop-skill/SKILL.md Case 3 实操要点 (自部署 != docker compose up; 生产用 K8s+Helm + 外部 PostgreSQL/Redis/Vector DB)
output/vllm-sop-skill/SKILL.md Anti-pattern 6 (vLLM needs 2+N CPU cores; under-provisioning makes API server the bottleneck)

operation_candidates.name: Throughput-headroom reality check (self-host)
operation_candidates.trigger: Self-host is chosen on cost grounds; you must confirm the self-hosted stack actually sustains the volume that justified it.
action: Find the per-replica ceiling of the platform you would self-host (e.g. Dify ~10 QPS / pod, node-level DB-query bottleneck).
Find the per-replica ceiling of the engine (e.g. vLLM continuous batching, KV-cache occupancy, preemption count).
Compute replicas needed = peak QPS / per-replica ceiling, then re-feed that replica count into OP-1's GPU+ops cost.
If the replica count to hit the volume is large, the 'self-host is cheaper at scale' assumption may not survive - re-run the crossover with the true replica count.
operation_candidates.output: A replica count required to meet volume, fed back into the crossover so the cost math reflects real throughput limits.
evidence: output/dify-sop-skill/SKILL.md §6.2 (~10 QPS/pod hard limit, 每节点单独 DB query)
output/vllm-sop-skill/SKILL.md OP-5 (triage num_requests_waiting, KV occupancy, preemption)

operation_candidates.name: Hybrid: self-host baseline + managed burst
operation_candidates.trigger: Volume is high enough that self-host pays for the baseline, but traffic is spiky, or a premium/compliance tier coexists with a bulk tier.
action: Provision self-host for the steady-state floor (high, predictable utilization keeps GPU $/token low).
Route burst / overflow traffic to a managed API so you do not over-provision idle GPUs for peaks.
Optionally split by tier: compliance-bound or premium traffic -> self-host; bulk/non-sensitive -> cheaper managed or a smaller self-host pool.
Keep one routing layer (gateway) so the self-host/managed split is a config decision, not a rewrite.
operation_candidates.output: A baseline-self-host + burst-managed routing plan that keeps GPU utilization high and caps over-provisioning.
evidence: output/vllm-sop-skill/SKILL.md Dilemma 2 resolution (serve two replicas - FP8 premium tier, AWQ free tier)
output/vllm-sop-skill/SKILL.md Step 7 (add a replica / data parallelism when SLA violated under load)

operation_candidates.name: Same-image / lock-in audit
operation_candidates.trigger: Evaluating a self-hostable platform's paid tiers, or worried about being locked into a managed vendor.
action: For self-hostable platforms: check whether Community / Premium / Enterprise are the SAME Docker image gated by env vars / license keys (then the open tier is technically sufficient for many needs).
For managed APIs: audit lock-in surface - proprietary model, proprietary fine-tune format, egress cost, contract minimums.
Prefer vendor-neutral self-host engines (vLLM, open weights) so the managed<->self-host door stays open both ways.
Confirm the license actually permits your commercial use before betting the product on it.
operation_candidates.output: A lock-in / license assessment for each candidate, plus the finding of whether paid self-host tiers differ from the open one in substance.
evidence: output/dify-sop-skill/SKILL.md Case 3 (Community/Premium/Enterprise 用同一 Docker image, 差异在环境变量)
output/dify-sop-skill/SKILL.md §6.4 (Apache 2.0-like license 'not really' - confirm terms before heavy commercial dependence)
output/vllm-sop-skill/SKILL.md §7 (TensorRT-LLM beats vLLM 30-50% but locks you in)

operation_candidates.name: Fallback / exit plan
operation_candidates.trigger: A decision has been made (either way); de-risk before scaling on it.
action: If managed: define the trigger that would flip you to self-host (e.g. monthly spend > V* for 3 consecutive months, or a new compliance requirement) and keep an open-weights equivalent identified.
If self-host: define the trigger that would flip you back to managed (e.g. ops cost / outage rate exceeds savings, team loses its DevOps owner) and keep a managed fallback wired into the gateway.
Keep the abstraction thin (OpenAI-compatible API surface on both sides) so the flip is a config change, not a migration project.
Re-evaluate on a cadence (quarterly) - price/quality frontiers and your volume both move.
operation_candidates.output: A documented bidirectional flip trigger + a thin OpenAI-compatible abstraction so reversing the decision is cheap.
evidence: output/vllm-sop-skill/SKILL.md §7 common pattern (develop on Ollama -> benchmark vLLM -> consider others) implies staged, reversible choices
output/dify-sop-skill/SKILL.md §1 判断公式 (bottleneck-driven, re-evaluable choice)

dilemma_cases.title: Low volume but data-residency requires self-host
dilemma_cases.rule: Compliance gate (OP-2) overrides the volume slider (OP-1). Even when volume is far below the cost crossover - where managed would be cheaper - a hard data-residency / air-gap requirement fails the managed path. Resolution: self-host the smallest adequate stack (single replica, modest GPU), accept the ops burden as the cost of compliance, OR find a managed in-region/BAA/VPC tier that satisfies the boundary (which re-opens managed). Do NOT pay for self-host scale you don't need just because compliance forced self-host - right-size it.
evidence: output/dify-sop-skill/SKILL.md Case 3 (合规/数据驻留, air-gapped -> self-host regardless of volume)
output/vllm-sop-skill/SKILL.md OP-4 caveat (multi-tenant privacy / cache salting)

dilemma_cases.title: Dify self-host vs cloud ops cost (the canonical case)
dilemma_cases.rule: Team <=3, small usage: Dify Cloud Pro/Team ($59-159/mo) beats hiring/diverting DevOps. High usage (millions of calls): subscription cost overruns infra -> self-host. BUT self-host is not 'docker compose up' - it is reverse proxy + HTTPS + backups + monitoring + upgrades, and production needs K8s + Helm + EXTERNAL PostgreSQL/Redis/Vector DB. And the ~10 QPS/pod ceiling means high volume needs many replicas (re-feed into the crossover via OP-4). Finding: Community/Premium/Enterprise are the same Docker image gated by env vars, so the open tier is often technically sufficient - the paywall buys support, not capability. Resolution: run OP-1 with the ops-labor term included and OP-3's honest capacity check; if either fails, managed wins despite a favorable raw GPU cost.
evidence: output/dify-sop-skill/SKILL.md Case 3 决策矩阵 + 实操要点
https://www.architjn.com/blog/dify-cloud-pricing-plans-free-tier-when-to-self-host
output/dify-sop-skill/SKILL.md §6.2 (~10 QPS/pod)

anti_patterns: Self-host for prestige / 'we run our own AI' at low volume - pays GPU + ops to lose money vs a $59/mo managed plan.
Choosing managed when compliance forbids it - a cost win that is legally void.
Costing self-host as raw GPU price only, with ops labor / backup / on-call / upgrades assumed free.
Assuming 'docker compose up' is production self-host (no reverse proxy, HTTPS, backups, external stateful stores, upgrade path).
Ignoring per-replica throughput ceilings (~10 QPS/pod, KV-cache limits) so the 'cheaper at scale' math never accounts for the replica count.
Over-provisioning self-host GPUs for peak traffic instead of bursting overflow to managed (idle GPUs invert the unit-cost story).
Paying for an Enterprise self-host tier when it is the same image as the open one gated by env vars - unless you actually need the support contract.
Making a one-way bet with no flip trigger and no thin OpenAI-compatible abstraction, so reversing means a migration project.
Treating the decision as permanent - not re-evaluating as volume grows and price/quality frontiers move.
